claim: Document processing is the critical bottleneck in RAG pipelines; handling multiple file formats (PDF, Word, audio, video) requires specialized extraction before chunking.
actionable: Use Docling for multi-format document extraction rather than building custom parsers; it handles OCR, table recognition, and format detection automatically.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Hybrid chunking using semantic similarity embeddings produces better RAG results than simple size-based splitting by keeping related ideas together across chunk boundaries.
actionable: Use embedding models to determine chunk boundaries instead of fixed token counts, allowing the model to decide where to split based on semantic coherence.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Converting diverse document types to a standard markdown format enables unified downstream processing for embedding and chunking across PDFs, Word docs, audio, and video.
actionable: Convert all documents to markdown as an intermediate format before feeding to vector databases; this simplifies chunking logic and improves consistency.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Speech-to-text transcription of audio files with timestamp metadata enables agents to locate exact moments in recordings when answering questions from audio sources.
actionable: Use local Whisper models in Docling to transcribe audio with timestamps; store both transcript and timing metadata in your RAG system.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Running document extraction and RAG locally with Docling avoids external API calls, enabling private processing of sensitive documents without data exfiltration.
actionable: Configure Docling to run entirely locally with HuggingFace models for document processing in environments with data privacy requirements.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Visual grounding in RAG systems allows agents to cite specific regions of source documents (drawing boxes over relevant content) improving explainability and auditability.
actionable: Implement visual grounding by tracking document layout information and coordinate data so agents can highlight source regions when answering.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Markdown is the ideal LLM-friendly format because it preserves structure (tables, lists, headings) while being simple text, unlike raw PDF or docx binary formats.
actionable: Always convert documents to markdown as an intermediate representation before vectorization and storage in RAG systems.
source_title: Turn ANY File into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS
